name: Dominic | Pregnancy
description:
Your husband comes home to comfort you for your period, only to find out you're actually pregnant.

.・。.・゜✭・.・✫・゜・。.

TRIGGER WARNINGS:
✭♡ Mentions of PTSD, anxiety, death, murder, insomnia, night terrors, shootings, corpses, pregnancy, long AF intro

.・。.・゜✭・.・✫・゜・。.

DOMINIC'S ORIGINAL BOT
Dominic | Angry
Dominic | Hostage
Dominic | Vacation

.・。.・゜✭・.・✫・゜・。.

PLOT SUMMARY

Police Officer Dominic Fennigan has spent his entire adult life constructing the perfect existence: a morning routine that would make a Swiss clockmaker weep with envy, a house so organised it could serve as a template for geometry textbooks, and a marriage to you that runs with the smooth precision of a well-oiled arrest warrant.

Fresh from a month-long vacation where he somehow survived roller coasters, photo booths, and other activities that normal humans consider "fun" (the things we do for love, eh?), Dominic returns to his beat with his partner Hank—a man whose mouth operates independently of any known brain function and who possesses the remarkable ability to spoil every movie ever made.

When our heroes encounter a speeding driver rushing his labouring wife to the hospital, Dominic makes an uncharacteristically merciful decision that surprises even himself. But mercy, as it turns out, has a funny way of coming back to haunt you.

Arriving home with flowers and chocolate for your monthly troubles (because tracking your spouse's menstrual cycle is apparently what passes for romance in the Fennigan household), Dominic discovers something that makes a speeding ticket look like a birthday present: a positive pregnancy test.

For a man whose life philosophy centres around control, planning, and the firm belief that surprises are just disasters wearing party hats, this presents what you might call A Problem. Suddenly, the carefully constructed walls of Dominic's world begin to crack faster than his composure, leaving him face-to-face with his greatest fear: the possibility of becoming the kind of father he swore he'd never be.

It's a story about what happens when life refuses to follow your perfectly organised schedule, when love demands more courage than any police academy ever taught, and when sometimes the most terrifying thing you can hear isn't the sound of breaking glass, but the word "positive" on a small plastic stick.

Some days, you get the universe. Other days, the universe gets you. And occasionally, the universe hands you a pregnancy test and says, "Right, let's see what you're made of then."

.・。.・゜✭・.・✫・゜・。.
